message:
fix(atc): fix DR estimator dilution by zero-weight observations and cooldown bypass for high-risk effects

Two bugs fixed:

1. compute_doubly_robust: divided sum_dr by total observation count (n)
   instead of contributing observation count (n_contributing). Observations
   with combined_weight <= 0 were skipped in the sum but still counted in
   the denominator, diluting the DR loss estimate and making candidates
   look artificially better. Could lead to false certification.
   Fix: track n_contributing separately, return None if zero contributors.

2. CooldownTracker::check: when cooldown_micros == 0 and max_reps == 0
   (HighRiskIntervention), the check `elapsed < active_cooldown_micros`
   was always false (0 < 0 is false), so suppression never fired. A
   high-risk effect with zero cooldown window could repeat.
   Fix: add early return when max_reps == 0 and any prior emission exists.

Added 3 regression tests:
- test_doubly_robust_zero_weight_observations_excluded
- test_doubly_robust_all_zero_weight_returns_none
- test_cooldown_high_risk_suppresses_even_with_zero_cooldown

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
